title: Document target command/state spec
description: Update analysis artifacts and project docs to lock the target command surface, .fabric state model, and doctor semantics before code deletion. Remove compatibility language for legacy ledgers, human-lock, .fabric/agents rule source, sync-meta public usage, and deprecated MCP write tools.
test: Docs/lint tests should verify public docs mention only init/scan/doctor/serve and target .fabric files; grep checks for removed public command recommendations.
acceptance_criteria: Docs clearly state: public commands init/scan/doctor/serve; doctor --json/--strict/--fix; .fabric/rules true source; events.jsonl only ledger; INITIAL_TAXONOMY retained; no human-lock/legacy compatibility.
hints: Use requirement.md as source of truth || .workflow/.lite-plan/wpp-20260426-doctor-command-surface-refactor/requirement.md;README.md;docs/getting-started.md;docs/initialization.md;docs/SPEC_INTERNAL.md;docs/CODEBASE_LANDSCAPE.md;docs/CONVENTIONS.md;docs/RULE_REGISTRY.md
execution_directives: Do not edit source code in this task except docs/tests if needed. Run rg checks for removed command names in docs.

title: Introduce rules-based meta and rule-test builder
description: Extract or replace sync-meta algorithms with a reusable builder that scans .fabric/rules as the rule source of truth, emits agents.meta nodes with content_ref, stable_id, layer/level, descriptions/sections, hash, and computes rule-test.index from @fabric-verify annotations. Do not expose a CLI command.
test: Unit tests for builder: creates meta from .fabric/rules; content_ref points to rules files; stale previous rule/test hashes are preserved; no .fabric/agents dependency.
acceptance_criteria: Given fixture .fabric/rules, builder returns/writes valid agents.meta.json and rule-test.index.json; no code path scans .fabric/agents for target-state generation.
hints: Reuse useful sync-meta helpers but move/rename to service. Study schemas first || packages/cli/src/commands/sync-meta.ts;packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts;packages/shared/src/schemas/rule-test-index.ts;packages/server/src/services/rule-sections.ts
execution_directives: Run targeted server/shared/cli tests for new builder. Keep same-wave write scope away from doctor command implementation where possible.

title: Refactor doctor service and CLI modes
description: Redesign doctor around target-state checks and report categories fixable_errors/manual_errors/warnings. Add --json and --strict CLI options. Make --fix call the rules-based builder, create events.jsonl if missing, rebuild bootstrap when deterministic, write baseline events, and never migrate legacy paths or check human-lock/audit completeness.
test: Doctor tests cover read-only report, json output, strict warn failure, fixable vs manual classification, --fix eliminating fixable drift, no legacy migration, no human-lock checks.
acceptance_criteria: fabric doctor reports target MCP readiness; fabric doctor --fix repairs only deterministic derived state; rerun doctor has no fixable drift; manual errors remain visible.
hints: Depends on T2 builder. Remove old audit/ledger/human-lock coupling from core report || packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts;packages/server/src/services/event-ledger.ts
execution_directives: Run doctor unit tests and relevant server tests.

title: Prune public CLI command surface
description: Remove public command registration and command modules/tests for update, bootstrap, config, hooks, approve, pre-commit, human-lint, ledger-append, sync-meta. Preserve necessary internal helper functions by moving them under init/service modules. Update i18n locale keys and snapshots.
test: CLI tests verify fabric help exposes only init/scan/doctor/serve. Removed command imports fail intentionally from CLI registry. Init still performs config/bootstrap/hooks setup internally.
acceptance_criteria: Public CLI surface is exactly init, scan, doctor, serve; init still installs integrations through internal helpers; no removed command appears in allCommands.
hints: Coordinate with T3 for doctor; be careful not to delete helper code init still imports || packages/cli/src/commands/index.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/init.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/config.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/bootstrap.ts;packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts;packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/en.ts;packages/shared/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts

title: Remove deprecated MCP write tools and legacy ledger/audit compatibility
description: Delete MCP fab_append_intent and fab_update_registry registration, tools, services, tests. Simplify server exports and tests to only current MCP tools. Remove legacy read paths/projections for .intent-ledger and .fabric/audit where they only exist for compatibility; keep events.jsonl as the only event ledger.
test: Server tests assert tool list excludes deleted tools; event-ledger tests cover current event types; read-ledger/audit compatibility tests removed or rewritten to event-ledger only.
acceptance_criteria: MCP server exposes fab_plan_context and fab_get_rule_sections plus resources only; events.jsonl is the only ledger/audit source for target state.
hints: Replacement event instrumentation already exists in plan-context/rule-sections/http JsonlEventStore || packages/server/src/index.ts;packages/server/src/services/event-ledger.ts;packages/server/src/services/plan-context.ts;packages/server/src/services/rule-sections.ts;packages/server/src/http.ts

title: Align init outputs, skills, templates, and dashboard with target state
description: Stop init from writing human-lock and .fabric/agents assumptions. Ensure init writes bootstrap, INITIAL_TAXONOMY, forensic, initial agents.meta, events ledger if needed, and installs skills/hooks internally. Update Claude/Codex skills to write .fabric/rules and init-context, then rely on doctor --fix for meta/rule-test derivation. Remove human-lock UI/API/docs/templates or mark out of scope for deletion task.
test: Init tests assert no human-lock output and no .fabric/agents generation. Skill/template tests/greps assert .fabric/rules and doctor --fix guidance. Dashboard/API no longer exposes human-lock approval if included in scope.
acceptance_criteria: New init target state matches requirement.md; initialization follow-up writes rules under .fabric/rules and init-context only.

acceptance_criteria: Repo consistently reflects target command surface and target .fabric state; no removed command/tool remains reachable; doctor --fix convergence is tested end-to-end.
